message:
bd-3mjjt.4: Wire evidence ledger capture during replay

Add evidence capture to the event trace system so Bayesian decision
entries are recorded alongside events and verified during replay for
determinism. Key additions:

- TraceRecord::Evidence variant with SerEvidenceEntry/SerDecisionDomain/
  SerEvidenceTerm serde types (owned strings for &'static str fields)
- EventTraceWriter::record_evidence() captures EvidenceEntry with ts_ns
- TraceFile::evidence_entries() extracts evidence from parsed traces
- EvidenceVerifier: compares replayed evidence vs recorded with epsilon
  tolerance for log_posterior, loss_avoided, bayes_factor, CI bounds
- 22 new tests (41 total in event_trace module)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3mjjt.1","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for Recipe D Deterministic Debugging","description":"## E2E Integration Test: Recipe D Deterministic Debugging (EV=recipe)

### Scope
Full integration test recording event stream, replaying deterministically, verifying exact frame reproduction, and logging all nondeterminism sources captured.

### Test Scenario
1. Initialize FrankenTUI with Recipe D recording enabled.
2. Execute a scripted interaction sequence (60 seconds of realistic usage):
   a. Type text in editor widget (50 keystrokes).
   b. Navigate between tabs (10 tab switches).
   c. Scroll list widget (20 scroll events).
   d. Resize terminal twice.
   e. Trigger theme switch.
3. During recording, capture all nondeterminism sources:
   - System clock readings (monotonic and wall-clock)
   - Random seeds (if any)
   - Terminal size queries
   - Any async event ordering
4. Save recorded trace to file.
5. Replay trace from file with deterministic substitutions.
6. Capture frame-by-frame hashes during replay.
7. Verify EVERY frame hash matches the recording exactly.
8. Run HDD on a deliberately broken replay to minimize the divergence point.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Recording events:
```json
{
  "event": "recipe_d_record",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "frame_id": u64,
  "event_type": "key|mouse|resize|timer|async",
  "event_data": "serialized_string",
  "nondeterminism_sources": {
    "clock_monotonic_ns": u64,
    "clock_wall_ns": u64,
    "random_seed": "hex_string|null",
    "terminal_size": { "cols": u16, "rows": u16 }
  },
  "frame_hash": "hex_string"
}
```
Replay verification:
```json
{
  "event": "recipe_d_replay",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "frame_id": u64,
  "recorded_hash": "hex_string",
  "replayed_hash": "hex_string",
  "match": bool,
  "divergence_point": "string|null"
}
```

### Success Criteria
- match == true for EVERY frame during replay (exact reproduction).
- All nondeterminism sources captured (no null random_seed if randomness used).
- Replay produces byte-identical terminal output sequence.
- HDD on broken replay identifies minimal divergence within 30 seconds.
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
- JSONL log is schema-compliant and parseable.
- Recording overhead < 5% of frame time.
- Trace file size < 1MB for 60-second interaction.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:26:28.454548494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:30.639566073Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mjjt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3mjjt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3mjjt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3mjjt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Format: one JSON object per event with {type, timestamp_ns, payload}. Diff algorithm: cell-by-cell comparison with mismatch reporting (row, col, expected, actual). Output: PASS/FAIL per frame with detailed diff on failure. Integration with insta snapshot testing for golden management. Must handle terminal size differences gracefully.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyCastle","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:01.034462875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T03:33:30.019106614Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T03:33:30.019089231Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mjjt.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3mjjt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3mjjt.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3mjjt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3mo","title":"Implement markup parsing for styled text","description":"PHASE 4/EXTRAS - MARKUP PARSER (Feature-gated)

## Background
Markup parsing allows writing styled text with inline syntax like [bold]text[/bold] or Rich-style markup. Feature-gated as it's not needed by all apps.

## Technical Requirements

### Markup Syntax Options

#### Option A: BBCode-style
[bold]Bold text[/bold]
[italic]Italic[/italic]
[fg=red]Red text[/fg]
[bg=#00ff00]Green background[/bg]
[link=https://example.com]Click here[/link]

#### Option B: Rich-style (Python Rich compatible)
[bold]Bold[/bold]
[red]Red text[/red]
[on blue]Blue background[/on blue]
[link=url]Link text[/link]
[#ff0000]Hex color[/#ff0000]

### Parser Structure

pub struct MarkupParser {
    style_stack: Vec<Style>,
    current_style: Style,
}

impl MarkupParser {
    /// Parse markup string into styled Text
    pub fn parse(&mut self, input: &str) -> Result<Text, MarkupError> {
        let mut spans = Vec::new();
        let mut current_text = String::new();
        
        for token in self.tokenize(input)? {
            match token {
                Token::Text(s) => current_text.push_str(&s),
                Token::StyleStart(style) => {
                    if !current_text.is_empty() {
                        spans.push(Span::styled(
                            std::mem::take(&mut current_text),
                            self.current_style.clone(),
                        ));
                    }
                    self.style_stack.push(self.current_style.clone());
                    self.current_style = self.current_style.merge(style);
                }
                Token::StyleEnd => {
                    if !current_text.is_empty() {
                        spans.push(Span::styled(
                            std::mem::take(&mut current_text),
                            self.current_style.clone(),
                        ));
                    }
                    self.current_style = self.style_stack.pop().unwrap_or_default();
                }
            }
        }
        
        Ok(Text::from_spans(spans))
    }
}

### Error Handling
pub enum MarkupError {
    UnmatchedTag { tag: String, position: usize },
    InvalidColor { value: String },
    InvalidAttribute { name: String },
    NestedLinkNotAllowed,
}

### Extension: Emoji Shortcodes
Optional: :smile: -> 😀, :rocket: -> 🚀

pub fn expand_emoji(input: &str) -> Cow<str> {
    // Fast path: no colons
    if !input.contains(':') {
        return Cow::Borrowed(input);
    }
    // Replace :name: patterns with Unicode
}

### Integration
// Direct usage
let text = markup!("[bold]Hello[/bold] [red]world[/red]");

// Via Text
let text = Text::from_markup("[bold]Hello[/bold]");

// In widgets
Paragraph::new(markup!("[italic]Styled[/italic] text"))

## Tests Required
- [ ] Bold tag parsing
- [ ] Color name parsing
- [ ] Hex color parsing
- [ ] Nested tags
- [ ] Unclosed tag error
- [ ] Escape sequences (\\[)
- [ ] Empty tags
- [ ] Link with URL
- [ ] Performance: 1000 char markup < 100µs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BBCode-style syntax supported
- [ ] All style attributes (fg, bg, bold, italic, underline)
- [ ] Link parsing with URL extraction
- [ ] Error handling with positions
- [ ] Escape sequence support
- [ ] Optional emoji expansion
- [ ] Feature gate: markup

## Location: ftui-text crate (feature = "markup")","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:37:44.886882212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:14:07.519175020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:14:07.519149231Z","close_reason":"Fully implemented in crates/ftui-text/src/markup.rs with BBCode-style parser, nested tags, color specs, proper error reporting. Feature-gated under 'markup'.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mo","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3monr","title":"Mermaid Compatibility Matrix + Fallback Policy","description":"Background
- Users need clear expectations about which Mermaid features are supported.
- Unsupported syntax must degrade gracefully with deterministic warnings.

Scope
- Define a compatibility matrix per diagram type (supported, partial, unsupported).
- Define fallback policy: warn vs error, degrade rendering, or show error panel.
- Map unsupported tokens to best-effort substitutes when possible.
- Define security policy: ignore HTML/JS, no external fetches, sanitize links.
- Define warning taxonomy and codes surfaced in JSONL and error panels.
- Integrate fallback policy into parser/IR validation and docs.

Considerations
- Consistent warning codes for JSONL logs.
- Deterministic behavior for unsupported constructs.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for fallback mapping rules.
- Snapshot tests for degraded rendering cases.
